hard_rules.message: Frame shampoo primarily as scalp and cleansing support, not as a length repair treatment.
Do not promise dandruff treatment, hair growth, or medical scalp treatment from shampoo guidance.
Do not infer color-safe, sulfate-free, fragrance-free, anti-dandruff, sensitive-scalp, repair, growth, shine, cadence, or protocol claims from shampoo names or brand lines.

Use normal shampoo for oil, sweat, freshness, and light residue; route stubborn buildup, waxy-coated feel after washing, hard-water/mineral suspicion, repeated dry-shampoo buildup, heavy styling residue, or nothing-gets-clean signals to reset guidance instead.
Saved or current routine add, remove, replace, or change requests for shampoo require routine mutation handling with build_or_fix_routine.
For persistent, painful, inflamed, wounded, spreading, unusual, severe, infection-like, shedding, or hair-loss-adjacent symptoms, route to safety guidance instead of shampoo optimization.

soft_rubrics.message: When useful, mention shampoo mainly at the scalp and rinse thoroughly.
When the user's goal is dry lengths, frizz, shine, softness, or split ends, answer the shampoo angle but explain that conditioner, leave-in, mask, or technique is usually the stronger lever.
Connect shampoo strength to scalp type, wash rhythm, buildup/residue, thickness, texture, and sensitivity rather than using a universal wash-less rule.

When care_balance_context.shampoo_cadence is present for a routine or shampoo-relevant answer, explain current versus target as a soft delta, prefer target_preferred, and pair any increase with scalp-focused washing plus length protection when dry or fragile lengths are visible.

required_grounding.when: Concrete shampoo names, recommendation order, named product detail, color-safe, sulfate-free, fragrance-free, anti-dandruff, sensitive-scalp, repair, growth, shine, exact cadence, or usage protocol claims are mentioned.
The user asks to add, remove, replace, or change shampoo in a saved/current routine, or the answer claims shampoo is already covered by the current routine.
The user reports stubborn buildup, waxy or coated feel after washing, hard-water or mineral suspicion, repeated dry-shampoo buildup, heavy styling residue, or nothing-gets-clean signals.
The user reports persistent, painful, inflamed, wounded, spreading, unusual, severe, infection-like, shedding, or hair-loss-adjacent scalp or hair symptoms.

ask_when.condition: Oiliness, wash frequency, residue, flakes, irritation, or dry-length mismatch would materially change shampoo strength or whether safety routing is needed.
ask_when.question_policy: Ask at most one targeted follow-up; otherwise give a conservative scalp-first shampoo answer.

ask_when.condition: It is unclear whether the user means ordinary oil/sweat/light residue or stubborn buildup, hard-water/mineral residue, repeated dry-shampoo buildup, heavy styling residue, or a nothing-gets-clean reset signal.
ask_when.question_policy: Ask at most one boundary-setting follow-up before treating normal shampoo as the right lane.

ask_when.condition: The user asks whether a named shampoo has color-safe, sulfate-free, fragrance-free, anti-dandruff, sensitive-scalp, repair, growth, shine, cadence, or protocol claims and product metadata is unavailable.
ask_when.question_policy: Ask for the exact product or state that the claim needs product detail grounding; do not infer from the name.
